title: RIO Execution Token
description: A short-lived, single-use token issued by the authorization service after a successful authorization decision. It is presented to the execution gate as proof of authorization for a specific action, as defined in the RIO Protocol.

token_id.description: A unique identifier for the token, formatted as a UUID. This ID MUST be unique for each token issued and is used for tracking and logging purposes.

authorization_id.description: The UUID of the authorization record that approved the action and triggered the issuance of this token. This creates a verifiable link to the authorization context.

request_id.description: The UUID of the canonical request that initiated the action workflow. This links the execution back to the original intent.

issued_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the token was issued. This is used to calculate the token's validity window.

expires_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the token expires. The execution gate MUST reject any token presented after this time. The expiration window SHALL be as short as reasonably possible.

nonce.description: A cryptographic nonce used to prevent replay attacks. The execution gate MUST ensure that a nonce is used only once.

scope.description: An object that precisely defines the action this token authorizes. The execution gate MUST validate that the attempted action matches this scope exactly.

action_type.description: The specific type of action permitted (e.g., 'wire_transfer', 'api_call').

target_id.description: The identifier of the resource or entity upon which the action is to be performed (e.g., a bank account number, a specific API endpoint).

parameter_hash.description: A SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized action parameters. This ensures that the parameters of the action at execution time match those that were authorized.

constraints.description: An array of conditions that MUST be met at the time of execution. These are evaluated by the execution gate.

value.description: The required value for the constraint to be met.

enforced_by.description: The entity or service responsible for enforcing the constraint (e.g., 'execution_gate', 'downstream_service').

issued_by.description: Information about the service that issued the token.

issuer_id.description: The unique identifier of the authorization service instance that issued the token.

service_version.description: The version of the issuing service, for audit and compatibility purposes.

token_hash.description: A SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized token content (excluding the signature itself). This provides a mechanism for quick integrity verification.

signature.description: The cryptographic signature that guarantees the token's authenticity and integrity. It is generated by the authorization service over the token_hash.

algorithm.description: The signing algorithm used, which MUST be ECDSA-secp256k1.

public_key_id.description: An identifier for the public key that can be used to verify the signature.

signature_value.description: The digital signature value, encoded as a base64 string.

status.description: The current status of the token. The execution gate MUST only accept tokens in the 'active' state.
